items.title: High Court to decide on Papagomo sedition appeal on July 31
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — The High Court today fixed July 31 to deliver its decision on the prosecution’s appeal against the acquittal of blogger Wan Muhammad Azri Wan Deris, better known as Papagomo, on a sedition charge dating back to 2023. Judge Nurulhuda Nur’aini Mohamad Nor set the date after hearing submissions from Deputy Public Prosecutor (DPP) Mohamad Mustaffa P. Kunyalam and defence counsel Muhammad Rafique Rashid Ali, representing Wan Muhammad Azri. Earlier, Mohamad Mustaffa argued that the charge against Wan Muhammad Azri was not defective, despite the first prosecution witness, Hisham Sharudin, who was also the complainant, having withdrawn his police report.

items.title: Maxis probes unauthorised access to customer’s account after social media post
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — Telecommunications company Maxis Bhd is investigating an incident involving a customer’s account that was circulated on social media, saying the matter was an isolated case involving unauthorised access. In a statement posted on its social media platforms today, Maxis said all access to customer accounts is fully logged and monitored. “Based on our findings, this is an isolated incident involving an unauthorised action.

items.title: AI shapes more shopping decisions: Adyen report
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Artificial intelligence (AI) is playing a bigger role in how Malaysians shop, with 74 per cent of consumers using AI assistants to discover products, find inspiration and help make purchasing decisions. More than half of Malaysians, or 52 per cent, said they are uncomfortable allowing AI to complete purchases on their behalf, citing concerns over security, transparency and control. Among AI users, 84 per cent said there is too much information on social media, while 74 per cent said AI helps them find shopping inspiration faster than other sources.

items.title: Malaysia sees rise in foreigners misusing social visit passes to work, says Immigration Dept
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — The Immigration Department has detected an increase in cases involving foreigners misusing social visit passes to work or conduct economic activities in Malaysia in recent years. Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said 1,279 arrests were made in 2023, rising to 2,644 cases in 2024 and 2,630 cases last year, Kosmo! He said 451 arrests had been recorded as of July 15 this year.

items.title: Sultan Ibrahim performs Hari Hol prayers at Johor royal mausoleum
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, visited the Bukit Mahmoodiah Royal Mausoleum here today to commemorate the Hari Hol of his late father. Sultan Ibrahim, who is also the Sultan of Johor, offered prayers and paid respects at the tombs of Sultan Iskandar Sultan Ismail, his mother Enche' Besar Khalsom Abdullah, and his son Tunku Laksamana Johor Tunku Abdul Jalil. The late Sultan Iskandar, the fourth Sultan of modern Johor, ruled the state from 1981 until his passing on Jan 22, 2010.

items.title: Burnham kicks off UK premiership with lucky breaks, investors hope they last
items.summary: BRITAIN'S new Prime Minister Andy Burnham appears to have room to juggle some popular economic policies without dropping the ball on the bond markets. While you may think that would spook creditors much in the way that Japan's Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's rise to power from within the ruling LDP late last year upended government bond markets there, that does not appear to be the case. Even though benchmark 10-year British government borrowing rates remain the highest of the G7 nations, they have stayed relatively calm near five per cent amid Burnham's spectacularly quick ascent since poor local election results in early May sparked calls for Starmer to step aside.

items.title: Myanmar factory worker charged with murdering compatriot in Klang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — A Myanmar factory worker was charged in the Magistrates’ Court in Klang today with the murder of his fellow countryman earlier this month. The accused, 32-year-old Maung Maung, nodded in understanding after the charge was read to him in Myanmar through an interpreter before Magistrate Nor Azilah Mat, according to a report published by Kosmo! According to the charge, Maung is accused of murdering Myo Than Naing at a premises along Jalan Tengah, Kampung Tengah Telok Gong, Port Klang, between 9.30pm and 10pm on July 5.

items.title: Bank Negara to continue promoting two-way flows in onshort financial market
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia will continue its efforts to promote two-way flows and deepen Malaysia's onshore financial market through the measured liberalisation of its foreign exchange policy (FEP) framework, while safeguarding Malaysia's financial and macroeconomic stability. Bank Negara Governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour said the central bank remains committed to strengthening the vibrancy, liquidity and efficiency of the domestic financial market through a combination of market development initiatives, sustained engagement with market participants and carefully calibrated policy enhancements, including the FEP framework. In an exclusive interview with Bernama, the Bank Negara governor said Malaysia has progressively liberalised its FEP framework over the years, providing residents and foreign investors with significant flexibility to undertake cross-border transactions and move capital, while maintaining prudent safeguards against risks from external borrowings and capital flows.

items.title: DBKL ramps up tree mitigation works to improve public safety
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) is continuing tree mitigation works across several locations in the capital as part of ongoing efforts to improve public safety and reduce the risk posed by urban trees. In a Facebook post today, DBKL said this week’s works will focus on the parliamentary constituencies of Bukit Bintang, Lembah Pantai and Batu. The works will be carried out at Selera Medan Bunus (Lestari Niaga), Jalan Pantai Murni in Pantai Dalam, Jalan Pantai Permai in Bangsar, Jalan Haji Salleh in Sentul, Jalan Sinar Sentul in Sentul, and the JPK Towing Depot along Jalan Sentul.

items.title: Social visit pass abuse rises as Immigration Dept intensifies enforcement
items.summary: CYBERJAYA: The Immigration Department has detected an increase in cases involving the misuse of social visit passes, with hundreds of foreigners found using tourist or social visit permits to work or conduct economic activities in Malaysia. Immigration Department director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said enforcement records showed a steady rise in such cases since 2023. He said the department recorded 1,279 arrests involving the misuse of social visit passes in 2023, followed by 2,644 cases in 2024 and 2,630 cases in 2025.

items.title: JP Morgan lifts Malaysia GDP forecast to 5.3pct, expects Bank Negara to raise OPR in Q4
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: JP Morgan has raised its 2026 Malaysia gross domestic product (GDP) growth forecast to 5.3 per cent from 5.0 per cent after the country's stronger-than-expected second-quarter economic performance. According to advance estimates, Malaysia's economy would have expanded 5.8 per cent year-on-year in the second quarter of 2026. JP Morgan said the performance marked a significant acceleration from the first quarter, when sequential growth had slowed to a three-year low of 1.7 per cent annualised.

items.title: Heavy rain, local factors caused Petaling Jaya flash floods, says Selangor exco
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — The flash floods that hit several areas in Petaling Jaya on Saturday were likely caused by exceptionally heavy rainfall over a short period, which exceeded the capacity of existing drainage systems, according to Selangor Infrastructure and Agriculture Committee chairman Datuk Izham Hashim. Izham said preliminary assessments also pointed to localised factors as contributing to the flooding despite various flood mitigation measures, including the construction of the FAS Tunnel. Izham said the state government would continue strengthening its flood early warning system to ensure residents receive timely information and have adequate time to prepare.

items.title: Energy, maritime security no longer isolated policy areas, says Fadillah
items.summary: IPOH: Maritime corridors are now a central component of national security as global trade, energy supplies and economic stability become increasingly intertwined with geopolitical developments. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said energy security and maritime security could no longer be treated as separate policy areas, given that disruptions to strategic sea lanes carry far-reaching consequences globally. He said recent developments involving the Strait of Hormuz highlighted how vulnerabilities at key maritime chokepoints could affect fuel supplies, electricity costs, trade flows and livelihoods.

items.title: MAA raises 2026 car sales forecast to 800,000 units
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Automotive Association (MAA) has revised upward its 2026 total industry volume (TIV) forecast to 800,000 units from 790,000 units, following stronger-than-expected vehicle sales in the first half of the year. MAA's revised forecast comprises 744,000 passenger vehicles and 56,000 commercial vehicles, compared with its earlier projections of 730,000 and 60,000 units respectively. The association also raised its forecast for all electric vehicles (EV) sales to 120,000 units, representing 15 per cent of the revised TIV forecast, from an earlier estimate of 100,000 units.

items.title: Great Barrier Reef at risk of 'collapse', scientists warn
items.summary: AUCKLAND: Australia's Great Barrier Reef is at a very high risk of extreme and widespread bleaching and "ecosystem collapse" linked to this year's El Nino weather phenomenon, top scientists warned Tuesday. Morgan Pratchett, a marine conservation ecologist at Queensland's James Cook University, told the International Coral Reef Symposium in Auckland, New Zealand, that the "extreme El Nino" posed a significant concern. Hard coral cover across the reef declined substantially in 2024-2025, with above-average water temperatures causing the reef's sixth mass coral bleaching event since 2016.

items.title: Removing any minister is PM's prerogative, says Zahid
items.summary: CYBERJAYA: The decision to remove any minister or deputy minister from office rests solely with the prime minister, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. Zahid said ministers or deputy ministers do not need to resign voluntarily if the prime minister has proof of disciplinary breaches. "The power of appointment lies solely with the prime minister," he said after attending the 104th Immigration Day celebration.

items.title: MK Land wins sustainability award for CSR, ESG efforts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MK Land Holdings Bhd has received the Community Empowerment & Environmental Conservation Award under the Company of the Year (Property Developer) category at the Sustainability & CSR Malaysia Awards 2026. The recognition also reflects MK Land's holistic approach to creating value beyond property development by balancing commercial success with meaningful contributions to society, environmental conservation and close collaboration with various stakeholders. MK Land group strategic communications and sustainability head Shahreen Rahim said the award demonstrated MK Land's belief that sustainable development was not merely about achieving business objectives but also about creating lasting value for society, communities and the environment.

items.title: Zahid outlines four mandates to transform Immigration Department
items.summary: CYBERJAYA: The Immigration Department must move beyond its conventional role as the country's gatekeeper and evolve into a strategic institution that drives economic growth, strengthens national security and leads digital transformation, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. He said the first mandate was for the department to become a facilitator of national economic growth through a more efficient immigration system capable of attracting foreign investment, global talent and tourists. On the second mandate, Zahid said the department must strengthen national security and sovereignty amid increasingly sophisticated cross-border threats.

items.title: Singapore-bound Malaysian workers urged to start journeys earlier
items.summary: To reduce delays at inspection counters, MCBA advised commuters to stagger their travel times and avoid the heavy rush-hour window. Those using the MyNIISe digital platform should ensure their QR codes are generated and updated before reaching inspection lanes, as updating at counters delays processing and worsens congestion. MCBA said digital border platforms would continue to be expanded, with manual counters gradually reduced in line with government digitalisation plans.

items.title: Matrix Concepts enters Hall of Fame for responsible development
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Matrix Concepts Holdings Bhd has been inducted into the Hall of Fame for The Edge Malaysia Responsible Developer Award, recognising its long-term commitment to sustainable township development. The Hall of Fame award is given to developers that have demonstrated sustained efforts in responsible development, including creating well-planned, liveable and sustainable projects that continue to generate value beyond completion. Matrix Concepts group managing director Kelvin Lee Chin Chuan said the induction reflects the developer's commitment to responsible township development and long-term value creation.

items.title: Police arrest man, seize RM100,000 Black-winged Kite in Kapar raid
items.summary: The operation was conducted by the Intelligence Branch of the Bukit Aman Federal Reserve Unit (FRU) alongside the Selangor Wildlife and National Parks Department (Perhilitan) under Operasi Bersepadu Khazanah at 9.40pm. FRU Commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Rosli Md Yusof said the 21-year-old man was arrested after failing to produce any authorisation documents to possess, keep, or rear the protected wildlife. "During the operation, authorities also seized a PVC pipe bird perch.

items.title: No need for anyone to resign, PM decides which ministers come and go, says Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — The prime minister has the sole authority to decide whether a minister or member of the government administration should be removed from office, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said today. According to Berita Harian , Ahmad Zahid said there was no need for ministers or deputy ministers to resign if the prime minister had received verified information about a disciplinary breach. His remarks followed Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s warning that ministers, deputy ministers and federal agency heads would be asked to step down if they used their official positions to attack unity government partners during the Negri Sembilan state election campaign.

items.title: Saifuddin: Malaysia to complete refugee registration by year-end
items.summary: Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the initiative, known as the Refugee Documentation Registration Programme (DPP), would produce a detailed database containing verified information on refugees currently residing in the country. He said the exercise was being spearheaded by the Home Ministry through the Immigration Department despite Malaysia not being a signatory to the 1951 Refugee Convention. "The Immigration Department today is not only dealing with immigration offences but also with highly sensitive issues such as refugee management.

items.title: Indonesian worker jailed six years, ordered whipped for child sexual assault
items.summary: TAWAU: An air-conditioner repairman was sentenced to six years' jail and three strokes of the rotan by the Sessions Court here today after pleading guilty to the physical sexual assault of his underage girlfriend at a rented room on July 9. Judge Ahmad Faizadh Yahaya handed down the sentence to Indonesian national Mansur Sudin, 29, following his guilty plea. The offence, under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, carries a maximum prison term of 20 years and whipping.

items.title: Ex-soldier charged with illegal possession of firearms and live ammo
items.summary: KANGAR: A former soldier was charged at the Sessions Court and magistrate's court here today with five counts of possessing a firearm, imitation weapons, and hundreds of rounds of live ammunition without authorisation early this month. Suhaimi Che Din, 49, faced four charges in the Sessions Court and one in the magistrate's court, all of which were read before magistrate Ana Rozana Mohd Nor. On the third count, he was charged with possessing 21 rounds of 12-Bore ammunition (12-Bore Buckshot) and 300 rounds of .22 Rimfire ammunition (.22RF Long Rifle).

items.title: Zahid backs review of Immigration service scheme after long wait
items.summary: CYBERJAYA: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has backed a proposal to review the service scheme for Immigration Department officers, saying the long-standing request deserves serious consideration by government. "I wholeheartedly agree that the request made earlier by the Home Minister, Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, deserves consideration by the Chief Secretary to the Government and the Public Service Department," he said in his keynote address at the 104th Immigration Day celebration today. The event was attended by Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Public Service director-general Tan Sri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abdul Aziz, Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail and Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban.

items.title: DPM Zahid denies claims of BN–Perikatan–GPS–GRS meeting, reaffirms support for unity government
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 21 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has dismissed viral claims that leaders from Barisan Nasional (BN), Perikatan Nasional (PN), Gabungan Parti Sarawak (GPS) and Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) were scheduled to meet today. According to Berita Harian , the BN chairman said he had no knowledge of any such gathering and stressed that BN’s position remains firmly aligned with the Unity Government until the 16th General Election (GE16). Zahid, who is also Umno president, said BN’s stance is consistent with resolutions adopted at the Umno General Assembly.

items.title: India's 'Cockroach' movement vows to protest until education minister resigns
items.summary: NEW DELHI: India's "Cockroach" party movement vowed Tuesday to press ahead with their campaign until the education minister resigns, a day after at least 180 people were injured in protests in New Delhi. Pradhan has to be sacked," CJP's Ashutosh Ranka said in a social media post. At least 178 people were injured in clashes between protesters and security officials, Delhi Police said.

items.title: Indonesia passes bill to set up international financial centre
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia's parliament passed a bill on Tuesday to establish an international financial centre, with the resort island of Bali previously floated as a possible location. Finance Minister Purbaya Yudhi Sadewa said the financial centre, known as PFII, is expected to draw foreign investment and help accelerate the country's growth. "If the national economy can expand and grow faster, this will yield significant long-term benefits for national development throughout Indonesia."

items.title: Bursa Malaysia lower at midday as it tracks Wall St weakness
items.summary: Among the heavyweight counters, Maybank fell two sen to RM11, Public Bank dropped one sen to RM5.18, CIMB fell eight sen to RM7.67, Tenaga Nasional gained two sen to RM14.32, and IHH Healthcare was flat at RM8.50. Pentamaster jumped 29 sen to RM5.20, and UWC bounced 27 sen to RM6.17. Petronas Gas fell 22 sen to RM17.40, Petronas Dagangan shed 16 sen to RM19.50, and Far East was 14 sen lower at RM3.80.

items.title: China summons Philippine ambassador over South China Sea encounter
items.summary: BEIJING: Beijing said it summoned the Philippine ambassador to China on Tuesday over an encounter a day earlier between the two countries in the Second Thomas Shoal, according to a statement from its foreign ministry. China and the Philippines traded accusations on Monday after an encounter between their vessels in contested South China Sea waters, with Manila saying one of its navy personnel was struck on the head and Beijing saying Manila provoked the incident. China's foreign ministry called the actions taken by the Philippines "egregious in nature."

items.title: Forest City: Network School, other tenants bound by Malaysian law
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI: Forest City says no tenant or operator within the development is exempt from Malaysian laws, regulatory oversight or licensing requirements. Master developer Country Garden Pacificview Sdn Bhd said it welcomed the government's findings on the investigation involving Network School. "Forest City has always operated in strict accordance with Malaysian laws, government policies and regulatory requirements.

items.title: Rights groups flag rising disinformation targeting Rohingya in Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — A wave of political disinformation targeting Malaysia’s Rohingya population has raised alarm among rights groups that say it is fuelling xenophobia and intimidation against the refugee community. More than 126,000 are Rohingya, making them the country’s largest refugee population. One Facebook post from a group called “Rohingya Malaysia” claimed there was a call for a Malaysian town to be handed over to a local refugee community.

items.title: 19 foreigners linked to Network School remain untraced, says Immigration Dept
items.summary: CYBERJAYA: Nineteen foreigners linked to the Network School in Forest City, Johor, have yet to be traced, with the Immigration Department investigating whether they remain in Malaysia or have left the country illegally. Immigration Department director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said the individuals were among 430 foreigners identified as being linked to activities involving the group. He said 210 of them were still in Malaysia, while 201 had been confirmed to have left the country.

items.title: IRC discussions will shape future AI laws, says Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Discussions at the 2026 International Regulatory Conference (IRC 2026) will be translated into legislation, with the Online Safety Act serving as a model for how policy deliberations can lead to legal reforms, Communications Minister Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil said. Fahmi said the government, through several committees, had drafted and subsequently tabled the Act in Parliament following earlier rounds of discussions. Fahmi said the Digital Ministry was leading efforts to draft the AI legal framework, although he acknowledged the process would take time.

items.title: Communications minister orders probe into alleged leak of influencer Khairul Aming’s personal data
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil has directed the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) to obtain a full report on claims that influencer‑entrepreneur Khairul Aming’s personal data was accessed and leaked without authorisation by individuals linked to a telecommunications company. The issue surfaced after a Threads user publicly claimed to know specifics about Khairul Aming’s phone bill, including an alleged RM498 outstanding balance and details of additional digital purchases. Khairul Aming questioned how such information could be obtained and demanded clarification from the telco.

items.title: Platforms cooperate but seek more time for under-16 social media ban
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Social media platforms are cooperating with Malaysia's ban on users under 16 but have requested additional time to fully comply, Communications Minister Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil said. Fahmi said the platforms were engaging with the government to ensure they fully understood the requirements under the Risk Mitigation Code (RMC) and Child Protection Code (CPC). "We see good signs among the platforms.

items.title: Son of Brazil's former President Bolsonaro secures US green card
items.summary: SAO PAULO: Former Brazilian lawmaker Eduardo Bolsonaro, son of former President Jair Bolsonaro and an ally of US President Donald Trump, told Reuters on Monday that he received permanent US residency, often called a green card. Bolsonaro has cultivated ties with US conservatives and the Trump administration. Eduardo Bolsonaro said he moved to protect himself from what he called unjust persecution by Brazil's Supreme Court, not help his father.

items.title: [UPDATED] RM19.8mil cannabis haul bound for Europe seized at KLIA, seven arrested
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Police have crippled six drug trafficking attempts through Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA), seizing cannabis buds worth RM19.82 million believed destined for Europe. The suspects comprise a local woman, four foreign men, and two foreign women. He said investigators believe the suspects were recruited by international drug syndicates to act as drug mules, transporting cannabis concealed in their luggage on flights bound for Europe.

items.title: Sabah mulls tax incentives for companies investing in downstream activities
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah is considering providing incentives for companies undertaking downstream activities in the state. State Finance Minister Datuk Seri Masidi Manjun said companies that intend to process their products within Sabah could be exempted from the State Sales Tax or charged a reasonable rate. Earlier, assistant state Finance Minister Datuk Ben Chong said Sabah collected RM1.387 billion in State Sales Tax as of June 30, 2026.

items.title: Accused of links to ‘love scam’ ring, nine Chinese nationals claim trial in Penang
items.summary: BALIK PULAU, July 21 — Nine Chinese nationals pleaded not guilty in the Magistrate’s Court here today to a charge of participating in a criminal conspiracy linked to a “love scam” syndicate earlier this month. Deputy Public Prosecutor Ikmal Affendi Zulkifly proposed bail of RM10,000 for each accused with two local sureties. The court granted bail of RM8,000 each with two Malaysian sureties and ordered the accused to report to the Sungai Nibong Police Station every Thursday.

items.title: Sultan Nazrin warns weak institutions, poor governance can undermine nations
items.summary: IPOH, July 21 — A nation may possess abundant resources, a talented population and a rich culture, but still fail its people if its institutions are weak, its leaders unaccountable, or its politics consumed by self-interest rather than public service, said the Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Shah. His Royal Highness said good governance in practical terms means the successful coordination of several essential elements in which institutions are strong enough to outlast any single leader or administration. “And it means a political culture in which disagreement is conducted with civility, the opposition is treated as a legitimate check rather than an enemy and power changes hands peacefully when the people so decide,” Sultan Nazrin said in his address at the 8th World Conference on Islamic Thought and Civilisation (WCIT): “Everyone Matters: Pride and Unity in Islam” here today.

items.title: King honours late Sultan Iskandar during annual ‘Hari Hol’ commemoration
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 21 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, visited Bukit Mahmoodiah Royal Mausoleum in Johor Bahru today in conjunction with the “Hari Hol” (death anniversary commemoration) of the late Sultan Iskandar. Accompanying him were the Regent of Johor, Tunku Mahkota Ismail, and the Raja Muda of Johor, Tunku Iskandar Tunku Ismail, according to a Facebook post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar’s official page, which also shared photographs of the ceremony. His Majesty also visited the grave of his late son, Tunku Laksamana of Johor, Tunku Abdul Jalil Ibni Sultan Ibrahim.

items.title: Kelantan police order station chiefs to make daily drug arrests under new strategy
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, July 21 — Police have introduced a new strategy to strengthen the fight against drug abuse by requiring every Officer-in-Charge of a Police Station (OCS) to make at least one drug-related arrest each day, effective July 15. Kelantan Police Chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said the directive was introduced after drug-related arrests declined despite intensified enforcement efforts. He said that during the first six months of this year, drug cases fell by 13 per cent to 13,957 from 15,051 cases recorded during the same period last year.

items.title: N. Sembilan polls see 141 campaign permits approved so far, say police
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 21 — A total of 141 out of 143 applications to organise political talk and campaign activities for the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election have been approved from July 18 to 20. Negeri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said all approved political programmes and activities were monitored by the Royal Malaysian Police (PDRM) to ensure that their organisation complied with the law and did not affect public safety and order. He also said that for the same period, Negeri Sembilan PDRM had received seven police reports and opened four investigation papers, two of which were opened yesterday relating to election offences.

items.title: Lucky escape: Hostel fire at Kulim religious school leaves 101 students unharmed
items.summary: Kulim Fire and Rescue Station Operations Commander Assistant Fire Superintendent Abdul Mois Ahmad Mansur said the department received an emergency call about the fire at 2.47am, Harian Metro reported. He said firefighters from the Kulim Fire and Rescue Station, assisted by personnel from the Kulim Hi-Tech Fire and Rescue Station, were dispatched to the scene. “The Kedah Fire and Rescue Department Operations Centre received an emergency call directly to the station, informing that a school fire had occurred,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain expected in nine states, Putrajaya until 1pm today, says MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected in several areas across nine states and Putrajaya until 1pm today, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). In an advisory issued at 8.50am today, MetMalaysia said the affected areas include Kuala Muda in Kedah; Penang; Machang, Pasir Puteh, Kuala Krai and Gua Musang in Kelantan; and Besut, Setiu, Kuala Nerus and Hulu Terengganu in Terengganu. The warning also covers Jerantut and Kuantan in Pahang; Klang, Kuala Langat and Sepang in Selangor; Putrajaya; Seremban, Port Dickson and Rembau in Negeri Sembilan; as well as Matu, Dalat and Mukah in Sarawak’s Mukah division.

items.title: Tengku Zafrul defends KWAP’s eFishery investment, says global investors were also misled (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — Datuk Seri Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz today defended KWAP’s due diligence in its RM163.4 million investment in Indonesian aquaculture technology firm eFishery, saying the pension fund was among several prominent global investors deceived by years of falsified financial statements. The senior political adviser to the prime minister said KWAP was a minority shareholder holding just 2.51 per cent of the company, and had invested alongside major institutional investors including Singapore’s sovereign wealth fund Temasek, Japan’s SoftBank, Indonesia-focused investment firm Northstar, and Abu Dhabi-based 42X Fund. Responding to questions over whether KWAP had carried out adequate due diligence before investing, Tengku Zafrul said the proposal had been assessed by the fund’s investment team before being approved by its investment panel.

items.title: Three killed after chicken-laden lorry crashes into parked trailer in Tanah Merah
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, July 21 — Three men died after a five-tonne lorry carrying chicken crashed into a trailer parked on the side of the road on the East-West Highway, Tanah Merah, early this morning. “The three victims were found trapped in the lorry. “The operation involved 12 firefighters with two engines from BBP Kemahang and BBP Tanah Merah,” he said.

items.title: Pahang asks agencies to review solution for Orang Asli land dispute in Kampung Sungai Cot
items.summary: KUANTAN, July 21 — The Pahang state government will request the Department of Orang Asli Development (Jakoa), the Pahang Land and Mines Office (PTG), and the Land and District Office to review assistance to resolve the land dispute issues of the Orang Asli community in Kampung Sungai Cot, Maran. Meanwhile, he said Pahang now has 264 Orang Asli villages with over 90,000 registered residents, including 30 villages that have been gazetted under Section 6 and Section 7 of the Orang Asli Act 1954 and Section 62 of the National Land Code. “The state government also approved the granting of land ownership to the Orang Asli community involving 16 villages under Section 76 of the National Land Code,” he said.

items.title: Melaka proposes temporary water tank to ease Kem Terendak supply woes
items.summary: MELAKA, July 21 — The state government, through Syarikat Air Melaka Berhad (SAMB), has proposed building a temporary water tank at Kem Terendak in Sungai Udang as a short-term measure to address recurring water supply issues at the camp. “The water issue at Kem Terendak falls under the purview of the Public Works Department and Mindef, but the state government, through SAMB, is committed to helping affected residents, military personnel and their families. He was speaking after the handover ceremony of two 5,000-litre water tanks from SAMB to Kem Terendak.

items.title: Man missing after boat capsizes in Kelantan’s Sungai Galas, SAR operation underway
items.summary: GUA MUSANG, July 21 — A man went missing after the boat he was travelling in capsized in Sungai Galas near Kampung Batu 6 here today, and a search and rescue (SAR) operation is underway. Gua Musang Fire and Rescue station chief Senior Fire Supt Muhammad Azrul Izzham Zulkifli said the victim was identified as Mohd Ilham Che Mat Satar, 27. Muhammad Azrul Izzham said five fire department assets, including a boat, were deployed for the operation, with search efforts covering more than 10 kilometres along Sungai Galas.

items.title: Man found dead with stab wounds in Kajang, police probe murder
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — A man was found dead with stab wounds to the abdomen in Bandar Mahkota Cheras, Kajang, yesterday morning. Kajang police chief ACP Naazron Abdul Yusof said police received an emergency call reporting the discovery of an unconscious man in the area at 8.45am. He said a preliminary examination conducted with the forensic team found injuries on the victim consistent with wounds inflicted by a sharp weapon.

items.title: Anwar apologises if remarks to civil servants came across as too harsh
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim yesterday apologised if his firm tone when addressing civil servants at various engagements was perceived as too harsh. “Many people have told me, ‘Anwar, you are now the Prime Minister. Anwar said his firm tone while delivering his message to civil servants reflected the importance of their role in implementing the government’s policies and national development agenda.

items.title: Pos Malaysia focused on cutting losses through transformation plan, says ministry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — Pos Malaysia Bhd, under DRB-HICOM Bhd, remains focused on reducing losses through its transformation plan, supported by network rationalisation, expansion of digital channels and prudent management of its cost base and revenue mix. The Ministry of Communications said Pos Malaysia improved its financial performance, reducing its net loss to RM19.5 million for the first quarter of 2026 from RM41.5 million in the same period last year. The ministry said this in response to Senator Robert Lau Hui Yew’s question on whether the government would provide financial assistance to Pos Malaysia given the company’s record of losses under DRB-HICOM.

items.title: Rapid KL says trespasser caused Shah Alam Line disruption, suspect detained
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — A temporary disruption on the Shah Alam Line this morning was caused by a trespasser on the tracks at Jalan Meru Station, Rapid KL said. Rapid Rail Sdn Bhd, which operates the Rapid KL services, said the incident was detected at about 6.15am, following which its operations personnel and auxiliary police officers acted quickly to locate and detain the suspect. “Rapid KL will provide full cooperation to the police to assist with the investigation,” it said.

items.title: MCMC calls in individual to assist investigation into alleged 3R post involving N. Sembilan Ruler
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 21 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) is working closely with the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) to investigate a case involving the upload of online content touching on 3R (Race, Religion and Royalty) issues concerning the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Ibni Almarhum Tuanku Munawir. “MCMC views the dissemination of 3R-related content seriously, particularly content that is provocative, insulting, incites hatred, or has the potential to trigger public tension,” the commission said in a statement yesterday. The commission also urged all parties to refrain from raising 3R issues, especially during the election campaign period.

items.title: Anwar congratulates new UK PM Burnham, pledges stronger Malaysia-UK ties
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has extended his congratulations to Andy Burnham on becoming the new Prime Minister of the United Kingdom, while vowing to elevate the Malaysia-UK Strategic Partnership. In a Facebook post yesterday, Anwar expressed his eagerness to work with Burnham in advancing bilateral cooperation, including finalising a Digital Trade Agreement and deepening collaboration in artificial intelligence, digitalisation, clean energy and defence. He also conveyed his appreciation to former UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer for his contributions to strengthening Malaysia-UK ties throughout his time in office.

items.title: AirBorneo apologises over MY5004 KL-Kuching flight delay due to technical issue
items.summary: KUCHING, July 21 — AirBorneo has expressed its regret over the delay experienced by passengers of Flight MY5004 from Kuala Lumpur to Kuching due to technical reasons. The Sarawak-owned airline reiterated that safety remained its highest priority, and explained that the flight departed at 7.30pm after the technical error was resolved. The airline apologised for the inconvenience caused and thanked all affected passengers for their patience, understanding and cooperation.

items.title: N. Sembilan polls: Pakatan manifesto focuses on deliverable promises, says Aminuddin
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 21 — Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) manifesto for the upcoming Negeri Sembilan state election focuses on deliverable promises rather than political rhetoric, said state coalition chairman Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun. Aminuddin, who is also the Menteri Besar, said the manifesto was formulated based on PH’s eight years in office in the state, with an emphasis on continuing existing initiatives while enhancing them over the next five years. Earlier, Aminuddin, who is contesting the Linggi state seat in the election, launched the coalition manifesto themed “Negeri Sembilan Kekal Harapan” (Negeri Sembilan Stays With Harapan), which outlines 10 key commitments.
